1θ1+2θ2+0θ3=1+260

θ2+2θ3=90 …… (2)
2×θ1+0×θ2+1×θ3=2+160
2θ1+θ3=180 …. (3)
and θ1+θ2+θ3=1+1+1θ
from (1)+(2)+(3)
3θ1+3θ2+3θ3=450⟹θ1+θ2+θ3=150
from (4) equation 150=3θ⟹θ=50 °C

subject: Physics
solution:
Expansion of hole will be similar to that of an imaginary disc.

Let diameter be d0 at 27 °C and d' at 177 °C. Therefore, we can write

d'=d01+αΔT

Therefore, change in diameter will be

⇒d'-d0=5×1.6×10-5×177-27

=12×10-3 cm

Hence, required value of d=12.

subject: Physics
solution:
Suppose m grams of water evaporate. Then, heat required
ΔQreq=mLv
Mass that converts into ice =150-m
Heat released in the process,
ΔQrel=150-mLf
Now, ΔQrel=ΔQreq
150-mLf=mLv
mLf+Lv=150 Lf
m=150LfLf+Lv
m=20 g

subject: Physics
solution:
The formula to calculate the rate of cooling of the object is given by

dTdt=KTf+Ti2-T0   ...1

For the first case, it can be written, using equation (1) that

80-605=K80+602-204=50K   ...2

If t is the required time for the second case, from equation (1), it can be written that

60-40t=K60+402-2020t=30K   ...3

Divide equation (2) by equation (3) and solve to calculate the required time.

420t=50K30K⇒t5=53⇒t=253 min=500 s

subject: Physics
solution:
Given:

∆Q=184×103 J,

m=0.600 kg at -12 °C,

S=2222.3 J kg–1 °C–1 and

L=336×103J kg-1

Heat required to convert ice from –12 °C to ice at 0 °C will be, 

Q1=0.600×2222.3×12=16000.56 J

Remaining heat Q=184000-16000.56

= 167999.44 J

Heat required to melt ice completely at 0 °C,

Q2 = 0.600 × 336000 = 201600 J needed which is more than Q, so 100% ice is not melted.

For amount of melted ice, we can write

167999.44=m×336000⇒m= 0.4999 kg

∴ mass of water =0.4999 kg

Mass of ice remaining = 0.1001

∴ Ratio =0.10010.4999≈1:5

subject: Physics
solution:
Newton's law of cooling states that 

T1-T2t=KT1+T22-T0   ...(i)

where T0 is the temperature of the surrounding. From equation (i),

60-407×60=K60+402-10

⇒207×60=40K⇒K=207×60×40

Let the temperature of the body after the given time be x °C. By Newton's law of cooling,

40-x7×60=K40+x2-10⇒40-x7×60=114×60(20+x2)⇒160-4x=20+x⇒x=28 °C

subject: Physics
solution:
By Newton's law of cooling
$$
\frac{\theta_1-\theta_2}{\mathrm{t}}=-\mathrm{K}\left[\frac{\theta_1+\theta_2}{2}-\theta_0\right]
$$
where $\theta_0$ is the temperature of surrounding. Now, hot water cools from $60^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ to $50^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ in 10 minutes,
$$
\frac{60-50}{10}=-K\left[\frac{60+50}{2}-\theta_0\right]
$$
Again, it cools from $50^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ to $42^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ in next 10 minutes.
$$
\frac{50-42}{10}=-\mathrm{K}\left[\frac{50+42}{2}-\theta_0\right]
$$
Dividing equations (i) by (ii) we get

$$
\begin{aligned}
&\frac{1}{0.8}=\frac{55-\theta_0}{46-\theta_0} \\
&\frac{10}{8}=\frac{55-\theta_0}{46-\theta_0} \\
&460-10 \theta_0=440-8 \theta_0 \\
&2 \theta_0=20 \\
&\theta_0=10^{\circ} \mathrm{c}
\end{aligned}
$$

subject: Physics
solution:
Total length at 30°C = 120 cm, each rod = 60 cm
$\alpha_{Al} = 24 \times 10^{-6}$/°C, $\alpha_{Steel} = 1.2 \times 10^{-5}$/°C
$\Delta T = 100 - 30 = 70$°C
$\Delta L_{Al} = 60 \times 24 \times 10^{-6} \times 70 = 0.1008$ cm
$\Delta L_{Steel} = 60 \times 1.2 \times 10^{-5} \times 70 = 0.0504$ cm
Total expansion $= 0.1008 + 0.0504 = 0.1512$ cm
Final length $= 120 + 0.1512 = 120.15$ cm

subject: Physics
solution:
Thermal stress in the wire is caused by contraction.
At -43°C, the temperature drop from 27°C is $\Delta T_1 = 70°C$
It creates tension $T = YA\alpha\Delta T_1$ where $Y$ is Young's modulus and $\alpha$ is linear expansion coefficient.
For tension to increase to $1.4T$, the new temperature drop must be: $\Delta T_2 = 1.4 \times 70 = 98°C$.
Required temperature: $T_3 = 27 - 98 = -71°C$

subject: Physics
solution:
Given here, mass flow rate of water, dmdt=2 kg min-1

The geyser heats the water, raising the temperature from θ1=30°C to θ2=70°C.

Total heat utilised in raising the temperature, ΔQΔt=dmdt×sΔθ

ΔQΔt=260×4.2×40=mt×L

L= heat of combination=8×103 J g-1

Now, rate of consumption of fuel is mt=2×4.2×4060×8×103

=4.26×10-3=0.7×10-3 kg sec-1

=42 g min-1

subject: Physics
solution:
A diatomic gas has 5 degrees of freedom apart from vibrational degree of freedom. For each additional vibrational mode, degree of freedom is increased by 2. So new degree of freedom,

f=5+2=7

Therefore, we require molar specific heat capacity at constant volume, which is given by

Cv=f2R=72R

solution:
Given,

Temperature of heat source, TH=103 K,

Temperature of heat reservoir, TL=102 K,

Conductivity of copper K=0.1 W K-1m-1

From the equation of steady-state heat flow,

dQdt=KA∆TL

Heat energy flux can be defined as the rate of heat energy transfer through a given surface.

1AdQdt=K×1000-1001=90 W m-2
